title: SDAP Handshake Confirm
description: The third and final message in the SDAP three-way handshake (Layer 2). Sent by the initiating agent to complete mutual authentication. By sending this message, the initiator proves it has received and verified the handshake-accept, echoes both nonces to confirm message ordering, and signals that the session is active. Upon receiving and verifying this message, the responder considers the session fully established and may begin accepting encrypted session envelopes.

type.description: Message type discriminator. MUST be exactly 'sdap/handshake/confirm'.

sessionId.description: The session UUID assigned by the responder in the handshake-accept message. Echoed here to bind this confirm message to the specific session.

initiatorDID.description: The DID of the agent sending this confirm message. MUST match the initiatorDID from the handshake-init and handshake-accept messages.

initiatorNonce.description: The nonce originally generated by the initiator in the handshake-init message, echoed here. The responder verifies this matches the init nonce to confirm the initiator has a consistent view of the handshake.

responderNonce.description: The nonce generated by the responder in the handshake-accept message, echoed here. Proves the initiator received and processed the accept message, completing the mutual confirmation loop.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this confirm message was generated. MUST be after the handshake-accept timestamp. Responders MUST reject confirm messages with timestamps more than 300 seconds from the accept timestamp.

sessionConfirmed.description: Boolean flag indicating session confirmation. MUST be true. The presence of this field as false would indicate an error condition and should not occur in a valid confirm message; use the error schema for rejection.

signature.description: A compact JWS signature over the JCS-canonicalized message body (excluding the 'signature' field), using the initiator's Ed25519 authentication key. The responder verifies this signature using the key resolved from the initiator's DID Document to confirm the confirm message is authentic.

delegationTokens.description: Optional array of compact JWT delegation tokens the initiator is presenting at session establishment time. These tokens assert the initiator's authority to request the granted scopes. Tokens MUST be validated by the responder before accepting any session envelopes that rely on those scopes.
